channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ADR Report Highlights Criminal Cases Among West Bengal Assembly Election Candidates
item.description: A recent report by the Association for Democratic Reforms (ADR) has revealed that nearly a quarter of the candidates contesting the ongoing West Bengal Assembly elections have declared criminal cases ...

item.title: Tanzania introduces Naltrexone in public hospitals to support alcohol addiction treatment
item.description: The Medical Stores Department (MSD) of Tanzania is planning to import and distribute Naltrexone, a drug used for treating alcohol addiction, to public hospitals across the country. This initiative aim...

item.title: Family in Homa Bay Seeks Justice after Son’s Body is Allegedly Taken from Morgue and Buried without Consent
item.description: A family in Ndhiwa, Homa Bay County, is in shock after learning that their son's body was taken from a morgue in Kisumu and buried without their consent. Job Otieno, who had travelled to Kisumu for wo...

item.title: Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred Launch Details, Patch 3.0 Updates, and Overwatch Collaboration Revealed
item.description: Blizzard has shared major details ahead of the launch of Diablo 4’s second expansion, Lord of Hatred, during its final developer stream. The expansion will officially release on April 28, 2026, but pl...

item.title: NDTV Yuva 2026: Event Showcases Young Indian Leaders and Voices
item.description: Looks like we've hit a bit of a hiccup, boss! The webpage itself is giving us an 'Access Denied' message. So, we can't actually access the live updates from the NDTV Yuva 2026 event. According to the ...

item.title: Why malaria remains difficult to control despite major scientific advances
item.description: Malaria remains one of the most persistent and complex infectious diseases, largely due to the adaptability of the Plasmodium parasite and its intricate life cycle. The parasite, which has existed for...

item.title: Understanding Pilates: Benefits for Mind and Body
item.description: Pilates is a well-regarded mind and body exercise method that originated in the 20th century, developed by Joseph Pilates. Unlike typical workouts focusing solely on strength or flexibility, Pilates e...

item.title: President Zardari’s China Visit Aims to Strengthen Economic Ties and Advance CPEC Cooperation
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari is set to undertake an official visit to China from April 25 to May 1, with a strong focus on boosting economic cooperation, trade ties, and progress under the China-Pakista...

item.title: Pakistan and Iran Discuss Regional Security and Bilateral Cooperation in Islamabad
item.description: On April 25, 2026, Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met with Pakistan’s Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Army Chief, Field Marshal Asim Munir, in Islamabad. The discussions focused on regional ...

item.title: Man sentenced to prison for rape in religiously aggravated attack against Sikh woman
item.description: A man has been sentenced to prison after being convicted of raping a Sikh woman in what prosecutors described as a religiously aggravated attack. The case highlights serious concerns around hate-motiv...

item.title: Filipina Jewelry Designer Expands Personal Collection into Celebrity-Favored Brand
item.description: A Filipina jewelry designer has successfully transformed a personal “birthday” collection into a growing jewelry line now worn by several celebrities. The collection, originally conceptualized as a cr...

item.title: US Senate to Vote on Resolution Limiting Trump's Military Action in Cuba
item.description: Chalo, listen up! It seems like things are getting heated again between the US and Cuba. The Democrats in the Senate are trying to put the brakes on President Trump, who’s been threatening military ac...

item.title: Trump sends US envoys to Pakistan for indirect Iran peace talks amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The Middle East remains on edge as the war involving Iran continues, with US President Donald Trump indicating patience in negotiations, emphasising he is in no rush to conclude peace talks. Special e...

item.title: Regulator orders LNG Canada to investigate and fix black smoke emissions at Kitimat plant
item.description: LNG Canada has been ordered by the B.C. Energy Regulator to determine the cause of black smoke emissions during flaring at its Kitimat facility and implement corrective measures. The order follows an ...

item.title: RAF Eurofighters intercept Russian drones over Ukraine near NATO borders
item.description: For the first time, Royal Air Force Eurofighter jets on a NATO patrol engaged and shot down Russian drones over Ukrainian airspace. The aircraft were scrambled from Romania following renewed Russian d...

item.title: B.C. Father Raises Alarm Over Online Network Linked to Daughter's Suicide
item.description: A British Columbia father, Jason Sokolowski, is raising serious concerns about an online extremist network known as 764 following the tragic suicide of his teenage daughter, Penelope. Sokolowski belie...

item.title: Indigenous Soldier Valentine Hare's Family Uncovers His Legacy After 100 Years
item.description: Valentine Hare, an Aboriginal soldier who served in World War I, was one of many Indigenous Australians who volunteered despite being barred from service. Although records were altered and his name ch...

item.title: SAHRC flags urgent service delivery concerns in Northern Cape municipalities
item.description: The South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC) has revealed that more than 70% of municipalities in the Northern Cape are at risk of requiring formal provincial intervention due to poor service del...

item.title: Index vs equal-weight investing: simple strategies for managing a $100,000 portfolio
item.description: The article explores whether investors are better off taking a ‘lazy’ or ‘really lazy’ approach when investing $100,000, ultimately focusing on passive strategies such as index investing. It explains ...

item.title: NASA Scientist Investigates the Possibility of a Hidden Fifth Force in Our Solar System
item.description: A new study by NASA scientist Slava Turyshev explores the possibility that a mysterious 'fifth force' could be hiding in our solar system. While Einstein’s theory of gravity holds up within our solar ...
